Scoresheet note when a queen is removed

Label above the archive panel title
Collapsible education panel title
en: What is the Eight Queens puzzle?

description: Opening paragraph. **..** = bold, *..* = italic
en: The challenge: place **8 queens** on a chessboard so that no queen can attack any other. A queen attacks every square on her row, column, and both diagonals.

description: Subheading in the education panel
Explains backtracking. *..* = italic
en: The computer places queens one column at a time. If a position is safe, it tries the next column. If it gets stuck, it *backtracks* — removes the last queen and tries a different row. This continues until all 8 queens are placed safely.

description: Subheading for the trivia list
Trivia item. **..** = bold
en: There are exactly **92 solutions** to the Eight Queens puzzle

description: Trivia item. **..** = bold
en: If you count rotations and reflections as the same, there are only **12 truly different** solutions

description: Trivia item. *..* italic, **..** bold. Berliner Schachzeitung is a proper noun
en: The puzzle was first published in the *Berliner Schachzeitung* in **1848**

description: Trivia item. *..* = italic
en: For an n×n board with n queens, finding solutions gets *very* hard as n grows. It's used to teach algorithms in computer science
